items.text: artbyalex 🌟 Just finished my first-ever DIY terrarium workshop at the Student Art Center! 🎨 Cost me $15 for the glass container and plants, but the instructor provided all the soil and pebbles for free. I learned how to layer sand, charcoal, and moss to create a self-sustaining ecosystem. Took about 2 hours, and I'm so proud of the result—it's now sitting on my desk in Anderson Hall! If you're into creative projects, definitely check out the next workshop on May 10. Do you prefer open or closed terrariums? Let me know in the comments! #diy #terrarium #studentart

questions.stem: According to the post, what did the instructor provide for free?

options.A: All the glass containers and plants

questions.explanation: The post states 'the instructor provided all the soil and pebbles for free.'

options.A: It will cost more than $15 to attend
options.B: It will likely involve similar materials
options.C: It will be held in a different building

questions.explanation: The post encourages people to 'check out the next workshop on May 10' after describing the terrarium project, implying it will be a similar DIY activity with comparable materials.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this post?

options.A: To advertise a paid workshop at the Student Art Center
options.B: To describe the cost of DIY terrarium supplies
options.C: To ask followers about their terrarium preferences
options.D: To share a personal creative experience and recommend it

questions.explanation: The post primarily shares the writer's personal experience creating a terrarium and encourages others to try it, rather than focusing on cost, advertising, or a specific question alone.

items.text:
Subject: Join the Campus Radio Station – Volunteer Recruitment

Hi everyone,

Are you passionate about music, news, or storytelling? The Campus Radio Station (WUCR) is recruiting volunteers for the Fall semester. We're looking for hosts, audio engineers, and content creators. No prior experience is needed—we'll train you during orientation on September 12 from 4:00 PM to 6:00 PM in Room 204 of the Student Union.

Volunteers must be available for at least one 2-hour shift per week. You'll get hands-on experience with broadcasting equipment and learn how to produce your own show. Plus, volunteers receive a free parking pass for the semester and a $10 gift card to the campus coffee shop after completing 10 shifts.

To apply, fill out the online form by September 5. Spaces are limited, so don't wait! Contact us at wucr@campus.edu if you have questions.

Best regards,
Maya Johnson
WUCR Volunteer Coordinator

format_metadata.subject: Join the Campus Radio Station – Volunteer Recruitment

questions.stem: According to the email, when is the volunteer orientation?

options.A: On September 5 from 2:00 PM to 4:00 PM
options.B: On September 12 from 2:00 PM to 4:00 PM
options.C: On September 12 from 4:00 PM to 6:00 PM
options.D: On September 15 from 4:00 PM to 6:00 PM

questions.explanation: The email states 'orientation on September 12 from 4:00 PM to 6:00 PM.'

options.A: Complete the online application form by the deadline
options.B: Attend the orientation session on September 12
options.C: Produce their own radio show during the semester

questions.explanation: The email says 'a $10 gift card... after completing 10 shifts.'

options.A: It is only for experienced audio engineers
options.B: It covers broadcasting and show production
options.C: It requires a fee to participate
options.D: It takes place every week during the semester

questions.explanation: The email mentions training during orientation and that volunteers 'learn how to produce your own show,' implying the training covers both broadcasting equipment and show production.

items.text:
Subject: Chemistry Lab Coordination – Experiment #3

Hi Jordan,

I'm writing to coordinate our next chemistry lab experiment (Experiment #3: Acid-Base Titration) scheduled for this Friday, November 10, at 2:00 PM in Lab 302 of the Science Building. We'll need to bring our lab coats, safety goggles, and the pre-lab worksheet from our last session. I'll also bring the $5 fee for the titration kit that we need to purchase from the stockroom.

Could you please check if we have enough distilled water and indicator solution? I think we're low on both. Also, we must submit the lab report by November 17 at 5:00 PM to avoid a $10 late penalty. Let's plan to meet on November 14 at 3:00 PM in the library to work on it together.

Let me know if that works for you!

Thanks,
Priya

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Jordan bring to the lab session?

options.A: A titration kit and distilled water
options.B: The lab report and a $5 fee
options.C: A lab coat, safety goggles, and a pre-lab worksheet
options.D: An indicator solution and distilled water

questions.explanation: The email states 'We'll need to bring our lab coats, safety goggles, and the pre-lab worksheet.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the lab report deadline?

options.A: Submitting it after November 17 will result in a penalty
options.B: It must be submitted before the lab session on November 10
options.C: It is due on the same day as the experiment
options.D: It can be submitted online through the campus portal

questions.explanation: The email says 'submit the lab report by November 17 at 5:00 PM to avoid a $10 late penalty,' implying that submitting after the deadline incurs a fee.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To remind Jordan about the lab experiment schedule
options.B: To discuss the cost of the titration kit
options.C: To coordinate tasks and plan for an upcoming lab
options.D: To request help with lab report writing

questions.explanation: The email focuses on coordinating the lab session details (what to bring, fee, checking supplies) and planning a future meeting to work on the report, making coordination the main purpose.
